rxn_class_summary: Membrane-free electrochemical epoxidation of chloropropene (and other alkenes) to epichlorohydrin via bromide-mediated bromohydrin intermediate over NiCo2O4 nanotip anode
electrochemical_role: Anode oxidizes Br- to Br radical (one-electron) which adds to alkene; anode also generates *OH from water oxidation; cathode reduces water to H2 generating OH- that deprotonates/eliminates HBr from bromohydrin

key_intermediates.name: beta-bromo carbon radical from Br• addition to chloropropene (ClCH2-CHBr-CH2• dominant; also ClCH2-CH•-CH2Br)
surface hydroxyl radical (*OH) from water oxidation

electron_flow: At the NiCo2O4 nanotip anode, Br- is oxidized by SET to Br•, which adds to the C=C of chloropropene to give a carbon radical; coupling with *OH (from anodic water oxidation) yields a bromohydrin. OH- generated at the cathode (from H2O reduction to H2) eliminates HBr from the bromohydrin to deliver the epoxide and regenerate Br-.
rate_determining_step: Anodic one-electron oxidation of Br- to Br• and its addition to the alkene (Br•/alkene radical addition step) over the nanotip anode

probe_reagents.name: BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene)
probe_reagents.observation: ECH yield in 0.1 M KBr dropped by >42%; yield in KCl essentially unchanged, supporting Br radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: 1,1-diphenylethylene (DPE)
probe_reagents.observation: ECH yield in KBr fell by >50%; DPE-Br adduct detected by HR-MS (m/z 259.0112/261.0091)

probe_reagents.name: N,N-diallyl-4-methylbenzenesulfonamide
probe_reagents.observation: Br•-trapped cyclized adduct detected by HR-MS (m/z 332.0137/334.0116), confirming Br radical

probe_reagents.name: DMPO (5,5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline-N-oxide)
probe_reagents.observation: EPR signals of DMPO-OH and DMPO-C adducts observed; HR-MS confirms DMPO-OH (m/z 130.0876) and DMPO-C with both [ClCH2C•(H)CH2Br] and [ClCH2CHBrCH2•] (m/z 270.1363/271.9733)

probe_reagents.name: H2-18O
probe_reagents.observation: Only 18O-labeled ECH detected by HR-MS, confirming water as sole oxygen source

notes: Alternative previously reported mechanism (Br/O redox synergistic, involving Br2/HBrO) is ruled out here: UV-vis shows no BrO- signal in KBr electrolyte, while ClO- is observed in KCl (consistent with Cl2/HOCl pathway). High-curvature NiCo2O4 nanotips enhance the local electric field to concentrate Br- and OH- at the anode; isolated Br adsorption sites suppress Br• coupling to Br2 (suppresses BER) and a high barrier for *OH further oxidation suppresses OER. NMR of bromohydrin intermediate shows ClCH2CHBrCH2OH : ClCH2CH(OH)CH2Br ≈ 2:1, indicating the ClCH2CHBrCH2• carbon radical is dominant.
